description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story agricultural building with a broad, pitched gable roof clad in reddish-brown corrugated material. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, giving it a rustic appearance. One side features a set of two rectangular windows with blue-tinted glass, while the opposite side is windowless. The front facade includes a large open bay supported by wooden posts, providing access for livestock or equipment, and a small section of wall with a single window. The roof overhangs the structure, offering additional shelter at the entrance. The overall design is functional and typical of rural or peri-urban agricultural buildings.
description: A rectangular wooden cowshed with a red gable roof, open front bay, and side windows, designed for agricultural use.
description_view_0: The front view shows the open bay supported by posts, a small section of wall with a window, and the wide red gable roof.
description_view_1: The side gable end is fully enclosed with horizontal wooden planks and no windows or doors visible.
description_view_2: The opposite long side displays two rectangular windows set into the wooden wall beneath the overhanging roof.
description_view_3: The rear gable end is similar to the other end, fully enclosed with horizontal planks and no openings.
attribute_door_style: large open bay with wooden posts, no visible hinged or sliding door
attribute_window_presence: two rectangular windows on one long side, one small window at the front
